content: {"pass": false, "issues": [{"problem": "Multiple substantive claims about EOAD genetics, autosomal dominant mutations (APP, PSEN1, PSEN2), mechanistic links to amyloid overproduction, and EOAD being a key/preferred population for mechanism-based and prevention trials are attributed to [1] but are not supported by the only ledger quote, which mentions only the age cutoff and 5–10% prevalence.", "fix": "Either add ledger items with quotes supporting the genetic, mechanistic, and trial-prioritization claims, or delete/soften those statements to stay within what the existing quote supports."}, {"problem": "Detailed descriptions of anti-amyloid, anti-tau, and other pathway-based therapeutic strategies (monoclonal antibodies, BACE/γ-secretase modulators, synaptic/neuroinflammation targets, prevention trials in presymptomatic carriers, biomarker work) are all tied to [1] without any ledger quotes showing that the source actually discusses these categories or their relevance to EOAD.", "fix": "Provide additional ledger entries with specific quotes from [1] (or other sources) that mention these therapeutic classes and EOAD-focused trials, or clearly mark them as general AD research directions not specifically sourced to [1]."}, {"problem": "The report ranks approaches as \"most advanced\" or \"emerging\" and asserts that anti-amyloid therapies are the leading disease-modifying strategies being actively tested in EOAD populations, but no ledger evidence supports these comparative or evaluative claims.", "fix": "Add ledger items that explicitly characterize the development stage and prominence of these therapies in EOAD, or remove the ranking language and limit statements to what is directly supported."}]}
